name: Serra d'Aire Loop
description: This difficult 21.6-mile (34.7 km) jogging route in Serra D'Aire offers rugged terrain, 5122 feet (1561 metres) of gain, and panoramic views
region_intro: Jogging routes around Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias traverse a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes and varied terrain, including the Serra d'Aire region. The area features challenging trails with notable ascents, offering runners opportunities to explore its natural contours. While many routes are demanding, the region also provides options for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias The most popular jogging route is Serra d'Aire Loop, a 21.6 miles (34.7 km) trail that takes about 5 hours 9 minutes to complete, featuring substantial climbs. Another top favourite among local runners is Fátima Roundabout loop from D. Nuno, a difficult 10.8 miles (17.4 km) path. This route offers a mix of terrain with moderate elevation gain. Local runners also love the Aire loop from Serra d'Aire Dinossaur Traceways, a 12.9 miles (20.7 km) trail leading through hilly landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours. Jogging around Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias is defined by hilly terrain, challenging ascents, and routes that explore the Serra d'Aire region. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 4 reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes in Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias?
A: The region is characterized by significant elevation changes and varied terrain, particularly within the Serra d'Aire area. Runners can expect hilly landscapes and challenging ascents, offering a diverse experience for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes for beginners in Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias?
A: While many routes in Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias are considered difficult due to the hilly terrain, there are a few easier options available. Komoot lists 2 easy routes and 33 moderate routes, providing choices for those new to running or looking for a less strenuous workout.

Q: Are there long-distance running trails available in the area?
A: Yes, Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias offers several long-distance running trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2187877768/serra-d-aire-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra d'Aire Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 21.6-mile (34.7 km) route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32169114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vale Garcia Ascent loop from Serra d'Aire Dinossaur Traceways&lt;/a&gt; extends to 36.9 km, ideal for endurance training.

Q: What are the typical durations for running routes around Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and elevation. Shorter, moderate routes might take around 1-2 hours, while longer, more difficult trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2187877768/serra-d-aire-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra d'Aire Loop&lt;/a&gt; can take over 5 hours to complete due to substantial climbs.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias?
A: Yes, many of the popular running routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19433426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fátima Roundabout loop from D. Nuno&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32204840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aire loop from Serra d'Aire Dinossaur Traceways&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenic views or landmarks can I expect along the jogging routes?
A: The routes often traverse the Serra d'Aire region, offering natural landscapes. Additionally, the area is rich in historical and religious sites. You might find yourself near landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3891272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basilica of the Most Holy Trinity (Fátima)&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2967545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Convent of Christ and Castle of Tomar&lt;/a&gt;, though these may require a slight detour from the immediate running paths.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias?
A: The running routes in Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Over 800 runners have explored the varied terrain, often praising the challenging ascents and the natural beauty of the Serra d'Aire region.

Q: Are the running routes in Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias suitable for families?
A: Given the prevalence of difficult routes with significant elevation changes, many trails might be challenging for younger children or families seeking a leisurely jog. However, the 2 easy and 33 moderate routes available could offer more suitable options for families, depending on their fitness levels.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the jogging trails in Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias?
A: Many natural trails in Portugal, especially in rural and natural park areas like Serra d'Aire, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially near wildlife or livestock, and to check specific route regulations if available, as some protected areas might have restrictions.

Q: Is there public transport access to the jogging routes in Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias?
A: Access to specific trailheads by public transport can be limited in more rural areas like parts of Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias and the Serra d'Aire. Major towns like Fátima or Tomar, which are nearby, have better public transport links, but reaching the start of some trails might require a car or a short taxi ride.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes starting near towns or popular attractions, designated parking areas are usually available. For more remote trails, you might find informal parking spots along roadsides. It's recommended to check the specific route details on komoot for parking suggestions or to look for parking in nearby villages.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias?
A: Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) generally offer the most pleasant conditions for jogging in Nossa Senhora Das Misericórdias, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, making early morning or late evening runs preferable. Winters are typically mild but can be wet.
tips:
From the top of the lake, amidst the lush greenery, you can see what was once the scene of giants: the Dinosaur Footprints Natural Monument stretches out nearby, revealing the grandeur of times gone by.

This is the highest point in the area, ideal for contemplating the vastness of the site. For those who enjoy running, the trails here are pure pleasure — to the north, dirt roads invite you to run at a smooth pace; to the south, the rocky terrain brings a touch of challenge. And the landscape, wide and serene, is the perfect bonus for every step.

The Garcia Valley hides a narrow and wild trail, where the bush almost blocks the path, but that is precisely what makes it special for runners. The classic climb is a real test for the legs: 200 meters of elevation gain in just one kilometer. Short, tough and full of charm.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this circular route?
faqs.answer: This challenging route is approximately 34.7 km long with significant elevation gain. For an average jogger, it typically takes around 5 hours and 9 minutes to complete.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Trail Serra D’Aire — circular route?
faqs.answer: The trail is known as the 'Stone Kingdom' due to its predominantly rugged and rocky terrain. Expect limestone formations, technical paths, single-tracks, and some muddy sections. While some parts may offer dirt roads, the southern sections are particularly challenging and rocky.

faqs.question: How difficult is this circular route for jogging?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult. With 34.7 km and over 1,560 meters of elevation gain, it requires a very good level of physical fitness and sure-footedness, especially given the technical and rocky nature of many sections.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect wide and serene landscapes, lush greenery, and impressive panoramic views across the Serra D'Aire. The route traverses the distinctive karstic landscape of the Serras de Aire e Candeeiros Natural Park, featuring dramatic limestone ridges, dolines, and caves. You might also spot dry stone walls and open fields, with views towards the Dinosaur Footprints Natural Monument from higher points.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several notable spots. You'll pass by the Cimo da Lagoa, which is a great viewpoint. You'll also encounter the Vale Garcia Ascent, a significant climb. The trail's location also provides views of the nearby Dinosaur Footprints Natural Monument.

faqs.question: Does this route pass through other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails within the park. You'll find yourself on parts of Trilho Vale Fundo, Trilho dos Escuteiros, Trilho do Vale Fojo, Trilho Varandas do Vale Garcia, Trilho da Estátua, and Trilho do Vale Garcia, among others.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations aren't detailed, the route is within the Serras de Aire e Candeeiros Natural Park. It's advisable to check local maps or park information centers for designated parking areas near the starting point of the circular route.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted in the Serras de Aire e Candeeiros Natural Park, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to jog this route. The temperatures are milder, and the natural landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder, wetter conditions, making the rocky terrain more challenging.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There are typically no entrance fees or specific permits required for general access to the Serras de Aire e Candeeiros Natural Park for activities like jogging. However, it's always good practice to check the official park website for any updated regulations or specific event requirements.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a challenging jog on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and technical terrain, pack plenty of water, energy snacks, and wear appropriate trail running shoes with good grip. A small first-aid kit, a map or GPS device, and weather-appropriate layers are also highly recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within a natural park, so facilities directly on the route are limited. However, you can find cafes and other amenities in the villages and towns surrounding the Serras de Aire e Candeeiros Natural Park. Plan to stock up before you start.
